text: The aim of this article is to try and contribute to the reconciliation process in South Africa. In the first section an attempt is made to understand what ubuntu means and how it can be described as a common culture or religion to the majority of South Africans across the barriers of language and other cultural factors. In the second section an attempt is made to describe the concepts of koinonia and diakonia which form the cornerstone of Christianity and where a large majority of South Africans believe in the Bible as the Word of God which teaches us how to live. The article attempts to relate the core values of ubuntu with the principles of koinonia and diakonia as they appear in the New Testament and to indicate ways in which the comparison of these values can contribute to the reconciliation of the people of South Africa.
